Plethora.text: Plaza full - a plaza completely full of a massive overabundance of items.

Pliable.text: Ply - like plywood, it bends easily and is highly flexible.

Poignant.text: Point - a sad memory so sharp it feels like it points straight into your heart.

Polemic.text: Polemic = debate weaponized: a blistering attack essay or speech against a person or idea.

Portent.text: Important - a highly important sign that something terrible is about to happen.

Potable.text: Pot - water safe enough to boil in a pot and drink.

Potentate.text: "potent" (powerful) = an incredibly powerful ruler or dictator.

Precipice.text: Press of ice - standing dangerously on the very edge of a massive cliff of ice.

Preclude.text: "pre-" (before) + "cludere" (shut) = shut the door before it happens, making it impossible.

Precocious.text: Latin praecox ("cooked before"): a child whose mind has "cooked" ahead of schedule.

Predilection.text: Pre-direction - you lean in a pre-direction because you have a strong preference for it.

Preponderance.text: "pre-" (before) + "ponder" (weigh) = an amount so massive it outweighs everything else.

Prepossessing.text: Possess - someone so attractive you want to possess their attention.

Presage.text: "pre-" (before) + "sage" (wise) = wise intuition that reads what is coming before it arrives.

Prescient.text: "pre-" (before) + "sci" (know) = knowing what will happen before it does.

Primeval.text: "prim" (first) + "ev" (age) = relating to the very first, ancient ages of the earth.

Privation.text: Deprive - a state of extreme poverty where you are deprived of basic needs.

Probity.text: Probe - a politician whose morals are so flawless they withstand the deepest legal probe.

Proclivity.text: Incline-built - in lean toward a habit; a tendency so steady it feels like gravity.

Profane.text: "pro-" (outside) + "fanum" (temple) = disrespect spoken outside the sacred space.

Profligate.text: Profits gate - throwing profits out the gate because you are reckless with money.

Profuse.text: "pro-" (forth) + "fundere" (pour) = pouring forth in massive abundant quantities.

Promulgate.text: Prom gate - announcing new rules loudly right at the prom gate.

Propensity.text: Pro pen - a writer with a natural tendency to always grab a pro pen.

Propitious.text: Profits - conditions look favorable; strong signs you could see big profits.

Propriety.text: Proper - acting with strict, perfectly proper, socially acceptable behavior.

Prosaic.text: Prose - writing that sounds like ordinary prose instead of poetry; dull.

Proscribe.text: "pro-" (before) + "scribere" (write) = write a name on a list to legally forbid someone.

Protean.text: From Proteus, Greek sea god who changed shape to escape - versatile, constantly shifting.

Prudence.text: Prude - so careful and cautious with choices that people joke you are a prude.

Puerile.text: Pure ill - a child acting so immaturely it makes you feel purely ill.

Pugnacious.text: "pugn" (fight) = eager to argue or fight; combative energy.

Pulchritude.text: Pull attitude - beauty so strong it pulls the room's attitude toward you.

Punctilious.text: Punctuation - obsessed with tiny details; corrects your text-message punctuation.

Pungent.text: Punch in - a smell so sharp it punches you in the nose.

Punitive.text: "pun" (punish) = intended to punish, not merely to correct.

Putrid.text: Puke - rot so foul it makes you want to puke.

Quagmire.text: Quack mire - a duck stuck in a muddy, complicated swamp.

Quandary.text: Wandering - wandering lost because you are stuck in painful uncertainty.

Quell.text: Quiet well - force quiets the riot well.

Querulous.text: Quarrel - whining and always ready to pick a petty quarrel.

Quixotic.text: Don Quixote charged windmills as giants - idealistic but impractical.

Quotidian.text: Quota - same boring daily quota; ordinary everyday life.

Rail.text: Derail - ranting so hard you try to derail the meeting.

Rancor.text: Rank core - bitter hatred sitting in the core of your heart.

Raucous.text: Ruckus - a loud chaotic crowd making a ruckus.

Raze.text: Razor - bulldozing a building down as if shaved by a razor.

Recapitulate.text: "re-" (again) + "capit" (head) = revisit the main heads or points in summary.

Reclusive.text: "re-" (back) + "clus" (shut) = shut away from the world; withdrawn.

Redoubtable.text: Doubt - an opponent so intimidating you doubt you can win.

Relegate.text: Relocate - moved down to a lower, less important rank or role.

Relish.text: Hot dog relish - savoring a moment the way you savor relish on a hot dog.

Remiss.text: Miss - careless with duty so you miss the deadline entirely.

Renunciation.text: "re-" (back) + "nuntiare" (announce) = announce you formally renounce a claim.

Replete.text: "re-" (again) + "plere" (fill) = filled back up to maximum capacity.

Repose.text: Pose - lying still in a calm resting pose.

Reprehensible.text: Apprehend - behavior so bad the police should apprehend you.

Reproach.text: Roach - scolding the owner for a roach in the kitchen.

Reprobate.text: Probation - a scoundrel who failed probation and slid back to trouble.

Reprove.text: Prove - scolding someone until they prove they will not repeat it.

Repudiate.text: Refuse it - reject a claim and refuse it as false.

Repulse.text: "re-" (back) + "puls" (drive) = drive an attack or feeling back.

Rescind.text: Send back - cancel an order and send it entirely back.

Resilient.text: "re-" (back) + "salire" (jump) = bounce back after a hard hit.

Resolute.text: Resolution - a firm resolution you refuse to break.

Respite.text: Rest bit - a badly needed rest bit during an exhausting hike.

Resplendent.text: Splendid - shining so bright it looks splendid.

Restitution.text: Restore - paying or working to restore what was stolen or broken.

Restive.text: Restless - a horse tense and restless, unable to stand still.

Retract.text: "re-" (back) + "tract" (pull) = formally pull back a false statement.

Revel.text: Rebel - the rebel parties all night after a wild win.

Revere.text: Reverend - deep respectful awe, like toward a reverend.

Revoke.text: "re-" (back) + "voc" (call) = call a license back and cancel it.

Rhapsodize.text: Rap song - praising or ranting with the heat of a passionate rap.

Ribald.text: Rip bawd - coarse, lewd humor ripped from the tavern; indecent and deliberately shocking.

Rife.text: Rifle - an area rife with rifles; common and dangerous.

Ruminate.text: Latin ruminare (chew the cud) - chew the same thought over and over.

Ruse.text: Confuse - a trick meant to confuse the enemy.

Saccharine.text: Saccharin - sweetness as fake as the sweetener; cloying and artificial.

Sacrosanct.text: "sacro" (sacred) + "sanct" (holy) = doubly sacred; not to be violated.

Sagacity.text: Sage - the deep judgment of a wise sage.

Salient.text: Sail - the main sail is the most salient feature on the boat.

Salve.text: Solve - ointment that soothes a burn and solves the worst of the sting.

Sanctimonious.text: "sanct" (holy) = acting holier-than-thou to claim moral high ground.

Sanguine.text: Latin sanguis (blood): old humor theory tied blood tone to confident optimism.

Satiate.text: Satisfy ate - you ate enough to satisfy brutal hunger.

Scathing.text: Scalding - a review that scalds; harshly critical.

Scintillating.text: Sparkling - wit and energy sparkling like champagne bubbles.

Scrupulous.text: Scrap - checking every scrap because honesty demands perfect detail.

Scurrilous.text: Scurry - ugly rumors that make the target want to scurry away.

Seminal.text: "semin" (seed) = a seed idea that spawns a whole field of later work.

Serendipity.text: Walpole coined it from "The Three Princes of Serendip" - lucky finds by accident.

Sinuous.text: Sinus - a road winding like sinus passages; serpentine curves.

Solicitous.text: Sole - hovering over whether your shoe sole is hurting your foot.

Solipsistic.text: "solus" (alone) + "ipse" (self) = the world is only "me" in your mind.

Solvent.text: Solve rent - cash flow strong enough to solve rent and pay debts.

Sophomoric.text: Sophomore - acting like a cocky, immature sophomore.

Spurious.text: Furious - the judge furious at evidence that is clearly fake.

Staid.text: Stayed - so staid they stayed home; serious, steady, unflashy.

Stoic.text: From the Stoa Poikile porch where Zeno taught - endure pain without theatrics.

Stolid.text: Solid - emotionally solid like a brick wall; unmovable.

Strident.text: Trident - noise as harsh as a trident slammed into stone.

Stupefy.text: Stupid - shocked stupid; brain freezes for a moment.

Subjugate.text: "sub" (under) + "jugum" (yoke) = bring a people under your yoke.

Succinct.text: Sucked in - speech with extra words sucked in; tight and brief.

Superfluous.text: "super" (over) + "flu" (flow) = extra detail flowing over the brim.

Surfeit.text: Surf it - so many waves you cannot surf them all; excess.

Surmise.text: Summarize - guess the whole plot from scraps, like forced summarizing.

Surreptitious.text: Syrup - pouring extra syrup when nobody is watching.

Swarthy.text: Weather-line - outdoor life etched into complexion (literary "swarth" tones).

Sycophant.text: Psyche + fawn - flatters power to win favor; a professional bootlicker.

Tacit.text: Take it - a silent deal where everyone takes it and says nothing.

Tangential.text: Tangent - barely grazing the topic; flies off on a tangent.

Tantamount.text: Total amount - equivalent in seriousness or value to another total.

Temerity.text: Team error - reckless nerve to blame the whole team error on the boss.

Tenable.text: "tenere" (hold) = an argument you can hold up under scrutiny.

Tenuous.text: Ten percent grip - holding the rope by the thinnest tenth; shaky.

Timorous.text: Tremble - timid and trembling like a frightened mouse.

Tirade.text: Tired - a long angry speech that leaves everyone tired.

Toady.text: Toad - creeping praise like a toad at the tyrant's foot.

Tome.text: Tomb - a huge tome as heavy as a tombstone.

Torpid.text: Torpedo - floating sluggish like a torpedo before launch.

Torrid.text: Tear it - passion so hot it tears at restraint; scorching heat.

Tortuous.text: Torture - path so twisting it feels like torture to navigate.

Tractable.text: "tract" (lead/manage) = easy to lead; docile and manageable.

Transgress.text: "trans" (across) + "gress" (step) = step across a moral line you should not.

Travesty.text: Tragedy - a mockery of justice so bad it is a tragedy.

Trenchant.text: Trench - criticism that cuts a deep trench.

Trepidation.text: Trap - dread that you are walking into a trap.

Trite.text: Tripe - an idea as tired as cheap tripe.

Truculent.text: Truck - aggressive like a truck ready to run you down.

Truncate.text: Trunk - chop at the trunk to shorten the tree.

Turgid.text: Turgid = swollen - bloated language or tissue, overstuffed and heavy (not muddy/turbid).

Turpitude.text: Moral filth - conduct courts label crimes of moral turpitude; deep baseness.

Umbrage.text: "umbr" (shadow) = take offense as if a disrespectful shadow fell on you.

Unctuous.text: Oil-on-tone - flattery so smooth it feels greasy; slick, insincere charm.

Undulate.text: Undulate - waves moving under the surface in smooth rolls.

Upbraid.text: Braid - scolding someone for ruining your careful braid.

Usurp.text: Use up - seize power to use up what is left of the kingdom.

Utopia.text: More coined "Utopia" from ou + topos ("no place") - a perfect society that cannot exist.

Vacillate.text: Vase - wavering forever between two vases you cannot choose.

Vapid.text: Vapor - remark so dull it evaporates like vapor.

Variegated.text: "varius" (various) = patched with varied colors or tones.

Vehemently.text: Vehicle - yelling with full heat at the car that cut you off.

Venerable.text: "venerari" (respect) = worthy of reverence for age, dignity, or achievement.

Venerate.text: Revere-plus - honor deeply as if before something sacred (same family as reverent).

Veracity.text: "ver" (truth) = truthfulness you can stake a claim on.

Verbose.text: "verb" (word) = padded with too many words; talky and windy.

Vestige.text: Vest - only a ripped old vest remains as a trace of the wreck.

Vex.text: Hex - annoy someone as if you cast a petty hex on them.

Vicarious.text: Borrowed thrill - living someone else's excitement secondhand.

Vindicate.text: Vindicare-show the accusation was false; clear a ruined name.

Vindictive.text: Victim - wanting the other side to become your suffering victim.

Viscous.text: Viscosity - thick, sticky, slow to pour.

Vitriolic.text: Vitriol - words as burning as strong acid.

Vituperate.text: Viper - hissing insults with viper venom.

Vocation.text: "voc" (call) = a career that feels like a calling.

Vociferous.text: "voc" (voice) + "ferre" (carry) = carrying a loud voice into the room.

Wane.text: Wayne - Wayne Enterprises lights dimming as Bruce fades after a brutal night.

Wanton.text: Wonton - wanton waste of a whole bowl of wonton soup on purpose; reckless malice.

Wily.text: Wile E. - cunning traps and scams like the cartoon coyote.

Winsome.text: Win some - charm that wins some hearts instantly.

Wistful.text: Wishful - nostalgia colored by sad wishful longing.

Wizened.text: Wizard - face wizened like a storybook wizard: shriveled and lined.

Zealous.text: Jealous - fire of a jealous lover turned toward a cause; fiercely devoted.

Zenith.text: Zenith tip - apex of the arc (sun at noon); your highest point, not the bottom below.

Zephyr.text: Zero fear - breeze so gentle it gives you zero fear of a storm.
